cpSummary: German lice-treatment buyers are overwhelmingly parents of school-age children reacting to an active infestation notified by kita/school. Repeat buyers stockpile for the next outbreak, and long-haired girls dominate the difficult-case complaints.

usageScenarios.label: Active infestation — first treatment
usageScenarios.reason: Bought immediately after lice discovered on child

usageScenarios.label: Kita/school outbreak notice
usageScenarios.reason: Treatment after receiving the standard 'Läuse' notice from kindergarten or school

usageScenarios.label: Repeat/second treatment
usageScenarios.reason: Second round 7-10 days later to catch hatched eggs or because first round failed

usageScenarios.label: After another product failed
usageScenarios.reason: Switched to this product after pharmacy/other brand did not work

usageScenarios.label: Precautionary stock
usageScenarios.reason: Kept in the bathroom cabinet for the next outbreak

usageScenarios.label: Whole-family treatment
usageScenarios.reason: Treated multiple siblings and parents together

csSummary: Sentiment is highly polarised: roughly 43% positive (4-5 star) vs 36% negative (1-2 star). The decisive issues are efficacy against eggs and the oily/silicone texture — when it works it is loved, when it fails customers feel robbed.

negativeTopics.label: Did not kill lice / lice survive
negativeTopics.reason: Live lice found hours or a day after the full treatment protocol was followed
bullets: Happens across all three treatment formats (shampoo, spray, serum)
Often after a single application even with full contact time
Drives most 1-star ratings and pharmacy-switching
quotes: "Unfortunately it didn't work for us. Even after 20 minutes of contact time, lice survived. I expected a bit more."
"Save the money, time and nerves and get a product from the pharmacy... applied it 3 times... lice are still there."
"I'm sure the lice are just stunned for up to 48 hours. Tested it twice on my whole family, and 1-2 days later the lice were back."
"After two treatments one week apart, new nits can still be found. A lot of money for no effect."

negativeTopics.label: Eggs/nits not killed — re-infestation cycle
negativeTopics.reason: Product kills adult lice but leaves eggs alive, so babies hatch a week later
bullets: Customers discover this only after the 2nd week when babies appear
Most products require a repeat treatment — many customers feel the label hides this
Leads to a repeating cycle that can last weeks
quotes: "It only partly kills them. The lice die but the nits (eggs) are untouched. A week later the baby lice have hatched and the horror starts again."
"Used twice as instructed — unfortunately eggs and nits survived."
"It reliably kills lice but against nits it is completely useless. You have to do multiple treatments, same as any other product."

negativeTopics.label: Oily / sticky / impossible to wash out
negativeTopics.reason: Silicone-oil base leaves hair greasy; requires 3-5 shampoo washes or washing-up liquid
bullets: Consistency described as 'castor oil', 'honey', 'craft glue'
Requires multiple shampoo rounds, sometimes dish soap
Particularly bad on long/thick/curly hair
quotes: "Horrible oily solution — covered everything in the bathroom and on my child's body, very difficult to rinse off."
"It spreads in the hair like craft glue. You have to wash it out with a lot of shampoo, at least two rounds."
"I had to wash her hair in the bathtub five times before it was acceptable."
"It is not a shampoo, it is an oily emulsion."

negativeTopics.label: Bad / flimsy comb included
negativeTopics.reason: Plastic combs too weak, teeth bend or fall out, too wide-spaced for nits
bullets: Teeth bend on thick hair or break off
Spacing too wide to catch nits
Several customers report the comb was missing from the package
quotes: "A tooth broke out of the lice comb and others bent — I'm no longer sure the shampoo is quality either."
"The comb that comes with it is terrible — the hair gets caught in the edges and gets ripped out. Better buy a proper one separately."
"Beware: the lice comb in the packaging had already been used and had dead lice on it!"
"The comb teeth bend on thick hair, rendering it useless."

negativeTopics.label: Messy / slippery floor hazard
negativeTopics.reason: Spray or oil drips off the head onto floor, making the bathroom dangerously slippery
bullets: Oily drops create a slip hazard — children and parents falling
Extra cleaning required afterwards
Spray format makes this worse — product ends up on walls and mirrors
quotes: "This stuff is LETHAL on hard floors. My little boy slipped, another child slipped, and I slipped over myself."
"Because of the silicone, the whole bathroom floor was covered in a silicone film — huge slip risk and a lot of cleaning."
"You have to put a towel under the chair because the floor becomes extremely slippery."

negativeTopics.label: Package / delivery problems
negativeTopics.reason: Bottles arrive leaking, combs missing, packaging already opened
bullets: Leaking bottles staining packaging and instructions
Comb missing from 'lotion with comb' variant
Several packages arrived visibly pre-opened
quotes: "When unpacking the shampoo I noticed the bottle was leaking at the neck and a lot had already run out. The instruction leaflet was completely soaked."
"The delivery came already opened and without the comb. Since we urgently needed the treatment I couldn't send it back."
"The package came damaged and the lice comb was not included — even though it's written on the invoice."

negativeTopics.label: Price gouging / cost concerns
negativeTopics.reason: Price tripled in a few months (15 to 32-34 EUR), feels like exploitation of desperate parents
bullets: Price rises of 100-120% reported inside 3-6 months
Customers who were loyal repeat buyers feel betrayed
Drives switching back to the pharmacy
quotes: "I bought the last bottle in early February 2026 for about 15 EUR. Now, only 1.5 months later, it costs almost 32 EUR. What justifies this?!"
"From 12 to 32 EUR in 3 months — that's pure cheek."
"In June it cost 15 EUR, now they want 35 EUR. Sorry, but no. Back to the pharmacy."

negativeTopics.label: Hair damage / dries out scalp
negativeTopics.reason: Hair brittle, breaks, knots; dry/irritated scalp after treatment
bullets: Dry, brittle hair that tangles and breaks even weeks later
Scalp irritation and eye burning reported
Particularly problematic for long hair
quotes: "Even weeks after the treatment my daughter's hair is extremely damaged, tangles badly and breaks despite conditioner."
"The stuff strongly dries out the skin."
"Three hours later my kids' eyes are still burning and you need 2 shampoo rounds to get the oily consistency out."

negativeInsights.finding: Most 1-star efficacy complaints come from customers who did only a single application and saw live lice the next day. The products work on adults only — eggs survive and hatch unless a 7-10 day repeat treatment is done.
negativeInsights.implication: Front-of-pack must say 'Zweitbehandlung nach 7-10 Tagen notwendig' and ship as a 2-application pack. Hiding the repeat step in fine print is the single biggest driver of negative reviews.

negativeInsights.finding: The dimethicone/silicone base is the active mechanism, but customers interpret 'oily' as a product defect. They blame the formula for hair fat and slippery floors, not understanding it is what kills the lice.
negativeInsights.implication: Reframe the oiliness as 'the physical barrier that suffocates lice' in pack copy and Amazon A+. Include a mandatory 'washing-up liquid first, then shampoo twice' instruction to prevent the 5-wash meltdown.

negativeInsights.finding: Long-haired, thick-haired, curly-haired girls are massively over-represented in negative reviews. One bottle is not enough and the comb bends in thick hair.
negativeInsights.implication: Offer an explicit 'Long Hair / Family' 2-bottle SKU with a metal comb, at a premium price. Mark the current SKU as 'short-to-medium hair'.

negativeInsights.finding: Slippery-floor incidents include children actually falling. This is a safety liability waiting to happen and some reviews mention near-misses and multiple people falling.
negativeInsights.implication: Add a prominent 'WARNING: Apply over bathtub or towel — oil on floor is a serious slip hazard' pictogram on the front of pack and in the listing's first image.

negativeInsights.finding: Reviews consistently accuse the incumbent (Nitwits/Evolsin) of price-gouging during outbreaks — 100-120% price hikes in 2-3 months. Loyal repeat buyers are publicly threatening to switch to the pharmacy.
negativeInsights.implication: A competitor entering with a <20 EUR stable-price 2-bottle pack and 'Fairpreis-Garantie' messaging can capture these defectors. The market is openly inviting a price-stable challenger.

negativeInsights.finding: The included comb is universally considered inadequate — too wide-toothed, too flimsy, sometimes missing. Customers routinely buy a separate metal comb.
negativeInsights.implication: Include a stainless-steel Nissenkamm as standard in the base SKU and make it the hero of product imagery. This is a cheap upgrade with outsized review impact.

positiveTopics.label: Killed all lice in one treatment
positiveTopics.reason: Fans report full clearance after a single correctly-applied treatment
bullets: Strongest driver of 5-star reviews
Often positioned as 'finally, after trying everything from the pharmacy'
Especially praised when pharmacy products had failed
quotes: "After trying many ineffective products, Nitnot finally got rid of the lice. With other products they were still alive after treatment — with this one everything was dead."
"I was so shocked to see lice but I'm happy I used this shampoo — it removed all my lice with one wash."
"Finally a product that works! Everything dead after one application. Absolute recommendation."

positiveTopics.label: Fast / short contact time
positiveTopics.reason: 15-20 minute contact time appreciated vs 8-hour pharmacy products
bullets: Huge advantage for impatient children
Beats Nyda's 8-hour contact time
Practical for working parents
quotes: "The promised 20-minute contact time really holds — the lice are truly dead afterwards."
"Short 15-minute contact is really practical with impatient children."
"I've used the spray several times when lice were going around the kita, and it has always reliably helped."

positiveTopics.label: Easy application (spray / simple)
positiveTopics.reason: Spray bottle or ready-to-use lotion is easy and comfortable for kids
bullets: Spray head makes self-application possible
Even coverage across the hair
Kids tolerate it well
quotes: "Very easy to use, just press the button and spray the hair. The smell is pleasant, not stinging."
"The handling is extremely easy, it smells pleasant and washes out relatively well."
"Simple to apply and spread, pleasant smell, kids thought it was much more comfortable than the pharmacy stuff."

positiveTopics.label: Pleasant / mild smell
positiveTopics.reason: Non-chemical, mild or neutral smell unlike traditional lice products
bullets: A clear point of difference vs old chemical products
Important for child compliance
Light citrus or neutral scent mentioned most often
quotes: "Smells not that bad and is easy to use."
"The smell is pleasant and the skin tolerance is super — no burning, no itching."
"Pleasant scent made the application more enjoyable."

positiveTopics.label: Gentle on scalp / skin-friendly
positiveTopics.reason: No burning, no itching, no irritation — safe for children
bullets: Important differentiation from old chemical lice killers
Highlighted as a key reason to trust it with kids
No allergic reactions reported by positive reviewers
quotes: "Neither my children nor I had any skin irritation or discomfort after using it."
"Great skin tolerance, no burning, no itching."
"It feels more like a care product, the hair becomes super soft."

positiveTopics.label: Works when pharmacy products failed
positiveTopics.reason: Last-resort purchase that succeeded after 25-70 EUR spent on pharmacy alternatives
bullets: Multiple customers explicitly compared to Nyda, Licener, Paranix, Rausch
Frames this as the product of choice after burn-out
Strong word-of-mouth driver
quotes: "I paid over 70 EUR for pharmacy products and still had live lice. This one worked in one application."
"After 5 kids and 3 years of recurring lice, I tried Paranix, Licener, Rausch, SOS Läuseshampoo. Only Linicin finally freed us for good."
"The best lice product on the market, in my opinion. Much better than the pharmacy competition."

positiveTopics.label: Good value / repeat purchase
positiveTopics.reason: Customers keep it as family staple and re-buy for next outbreak
bullets: Stockpiling behaviour — always one in the cabinet
Recommendation to friends and family
Framed as cheaper than pharmacy
quotes: "For me it's now the only lice product I still buy."
"I always keep one at home as reserve, in case another lice note comes from school."
"Much cheaper and more effective than the pharmacy product."

positiveInsights.finding: The happiest customers explicitly compare this product to pharmacy brands (Nyda, Licener, Paranix, Rausch) that failed. Positive reviews read like 'we tried everything, finally this worked'.
positiveInsights.implication: Position Amazon listings as the 'after-pharmacy' solution. A+ content should name the scenario: 'Pharmacy product didn't work? Here's why silicone oil succeeds where chemical treatments fail.'

positiveInsights.finding: Short contact time (15-20 min) is a consistent, strong differentiator vs the 8-hour Nyda standard. Working parents and impatient kids are an under-served segment.
positiveInsights.implication: Lead hero image with a clear '20 Min' badge. Targeted paid media: 'Lice treatment in 20 minutes, not 8 hours.'

positiveInsights.finding: Skin tolerance and 'no burning' are repeatedly cited as trust triggers. Parents remember the old chemical products ('the one with fire on the packaging') and are relieved by the gentle formulas.
positiveInsights.implication: Feature pediatric-friendly cues: 'Hautfreundlich, bienenstichfrei, ohne brennende Chemie'. Consider dermatological-tested certification.

positiveInsights.finding: Customers who succeeded followed the instructions precisely — full head coverage, full contact time, and a repeat treatment after a week. Those who failed skipped the repeat.
positiveInsights.implication: A QR code on pack to a 60-second application video would convert a large chunk of the failure reviews into successes. Low cost, high leverage.

positiveInsights.finding: Repeat buyers describe themselves as 'keeping one in the cabinet' — they are stockpiling for the next kita outbreak. This is strong retention behaviour in a category most people think of as one-off.
positiveInsights.implication: Launch a 'Läuse-Vorrat' 2-pack SKU or a Spar-Abo subscription with a 10-15% discount. Capture the stockpile moment explicitly.

positiveInsights.finding: Pleasant/mild smell is one of the most repeated positives and directly contrasts the 'stinging chemical' memory. Scent is an under-leveraged differentiator.
positiveInsights.implication: Develop a lightly scented 'Kinder-Edition' with a subtle citrus or chamomile profile. Use scent as a primary packaging cue.

buyersMotivation.label: Eliminate active infestation fast
buyersMotivation.reason: Child already has lice and needs to return to school/kita

buyersMotivation.label: Switch from failed pharmacy product
buyersMotivation.reason: Previous treatment (Nyda, Paranix, Licener etc.) did not work

buyersMotivation.label: Gentler/child-friendly alternative
buyersMotivation.reason: Avoid the stinging chemical treatments from the past

buyersMotivation.label: Short contact time with impatient kids
Stockpile for next outbreak
buyersMotivation.reason: Keep one bottle in the cabinet as a reserve

customerExpectations.label: One-and-done treatment
customerExpectations.reason: Customers expect a single application to kill lice AND eggs

customerExpectations.label: Easy to wash out
customerExpectations.reason: Expect normal 1-2 shampoo washes, not 3-5 rounds with dish soap

customerExpectations.label: A proper metal nit comb included
customerExpectations.reason: Expect a functional, fine-toothed comb that actually removes nits

customerExpectations.label: Truly kills nits (eggs)
customerExpectations.reason: Expect 'Läuse und Nissen' claim to mean both are killed, not just adults

customerExpectations.label: Non-oily / shampoo-like texture
customerExpectations.reason: Product marketed as shampoo is expected to foam and behave like shampoo

customerExpectations.label: Stable fair price
customerExpectations.reason: Expect repeat-buy price to be roughly constant, not doubled during outbreaks

customerExpectations.label: Full coverage in one bottle
customerExpectations.reason: Expect one bottle to be enough for long/thick hair and for two applications

reviews.t: When unpacking the shampoo I noticed the bottle was leaking at the neck and a lot of product had run out. The instruction leaflet was completely soaked in oily liquid. Since the product isn't cheap, I returned it straight away.

Horrible oily solution. Not sure the product even worked, but the worst is the consistency — calling it a shampoo is a mystery. It's a very oily solution that covered everything in the bathroom and my child's body, very hard to rinse off, needed multiple soap washes to remove, ran over my toddler's face. Absolutely not for little ones.
Warning: the lice comb in the packaging had already been used and there were dead lice on it!
Save your money, time and nerves and get a product from the pharmacy. Applied this 3 times — lice are still there.
Unfortunately it didn't work for us. Despite the 20-minute contact time, lice survived. I expected more.
Parts missing — the comb was missing.
Disgusting, unbelievable — I opened the packaging and the lice comb was used, full of lice and hair.

Absolute disappointment. Doesn't work at all. Applied it on 2 consecutive days because live lice were still there after the first time, and even after 2 applications still some alive. Total waste of money.
Never again. Terrible, won't spread at all, very oily and doesn't help either.
The comb is far too soft — not suitable for lice.

A bit weak. Effectiveness is not 100%. I found a live louse 4 days later, though most were killed. Helped me in an emergency, so I think it's only a complementary treatment since it has no strong smell.
Doesn't really help. Application is easy but despite long contact time and spraying everything, most lice were still alive afterwards. The comb is however quite good.
The lice comb was missing from the delivery. Now I have to go back and forth with messages. Hmm.

Quality questionable. After a tooth broke off the lice comb and the others partly bent, I'm no longer sure whether the shampoo itself is any good. I'm getting everything from the pharmacy just to be safe.
Didn't help. Even after several applications no improvement. Can't recommend it.
There are better ones. The lotion is very thick, you can barely comb through, and you rip out lots of hair.
It didn't work for me, just like other treatments.
Too sticky — you have to wash hair 3 times to de-grease it.

It's not a shampoo. Not sure if what was delivered was expired, but it's advertised as shampoo and doesn't foam with or without water. It's just a greasy liquid. I would have returned it if I hadn't already used it once.
Misleading name! It's not a shampoo, it's an oily emulsion, though it does wash out relatively well. Effectiveness still to be tested.
Unfortunately didn't help us. Shipping, application, comb — all okay.

Effective yes, but... I can only partly recommend the shampoo because it's hard to wash out. It does what it should and is effective against head lice, but even after thorough washing and shampooing with normal shampoo, the hair still looks oily. Difficult when the child doesn't like having hair washed. For us the best lice shampoo is Mediker — a gel, works in 3-5 minutes and washes out super.
Meh. Product is good, comb is bad. The metal tines fell out during use.

Helps only partially. It gets out the sticky nits (comb a bit rough, spacing too big) but you have to catch everything. Afterwards there are still yellow nits in the hair. With another brand the nits are brown = dead afterwards. Here not.

Meh. Only partly kills. Some lice still survive and the nits (eggs) are untouched. A week later the baby lice have hatched and the cycle starts again. You have to keep going, treating hair weekly. Can last up to 8 weeks. Not effective enough!!!
It works but damages hair. Even weeks after the treatment the hair is extremely damaged and hard to comb despite conditioner, tangles and breaks.
Result good. No further comment possible.
OK but no shampoo. Works like every other lice oil. 2 stars off because they call it SHAMPOO — that's a deception. It's the same oily stuff as other products.
Bad explanation. The lice shampoo does its job but the instructions are poor. The included comb quality could be improved. Overall satisfactory but with room for improvement.
Fast delivery as described. Bought as a precaution, product is as described.
Does it work? It didn't help us as described. After several applications we had to use another product.
Lice be gone. Use a lot and at least 2 times.
Top.
It helped. Always use lots.
Good product, but the included comb is not really fine. With a different one I could comb out the dead lice better.
OK — usable.
Helped great. Despite two shampoo rounds it leaves an oily film. But it killed the lice.

Very thick when applying. I had hoped for a shampoo that feels and spreads normally so that applying on the scalp is easy. The effect was great, doesn't smell strong, but handling is hard because it's so thick.
Works well.
Good work. Smells not that bad and is easy to use.
Works well. Fast delivery.
Lice — works perfectly.
Good product!

An invaluable product against lice. The Evolsin shampoo is incredibly effective — removed lice and nits on my children and me in no time. The included lice comb was very helpful. Gentle on the scalp — no irritation. Pleasant fragrance, long-lasting protection against re-infestation. Warmly recommended.
Good product. Helped well and especially the metal comb is very useful.

Clear recommendation! I bought the Evolsin shampoo & comb set and am really satisfied. Application very easy and well explained. Shampoo worked well and was gentle on the scalp, no unpleasant smell. The comb is stable and helps remove nits.
Goodbye critters. Very oily — 2 days of hair washing if you use a bit too much. It helped us.
Lice shampoo & comb — fast delivery and very good.
Very good — does its job.
Great. Gentle, does what it should.
Kindergarten child — bought as safety net for the next kita outbreak.

I hadn't had lice in almost 15 years and was so shocked to see lice and so many nits. Happy I used this shampoo — removed all lice in one wash. A week later I couldn't find any lice or nits. Feeling so relieved.

Works very well against lice. Simple to apply. The included comb is very good. We took Evolsin to Cuba — the grandmother of our granddaughters was thrilled. Helpful that the instructions are also in Spanish.
Top. Great stuff.

What happened? In October 2025 I bought this on subscribe & save for 13.50 EUR, now it's almost 20 EUR more?! Product itself is actually great but a price increase like this in 6 months is just too much. One star.
Ineffective. Easy to apply but unfortunately does not convince at all on efficacy. After two treatments one week apart, new nits are still found. A lot of money for nothing.
Too expensive. Was good but went from 12 to 32 EUR in 3 months — pure cheek.
NitWits All-in-One Lice Spray — unfortunately the bottle arrived half empty because it had leaked.
No great results. Overrated. Had to buy other brands afterwards.

Not worth the money. Go to the nearest Apotheke instead. I just used the Nitwits spray today. Followed the instructions, rinsed the hair, used the lice comb — and there was a big fully-grown live louse still moving. Not buying this again.

Didn't get rid of all lice. We're super disappointed. Paid 30 EUR for 2 bottles. 2 months ago we had a school lice outbreak and bought a product at the pharmacy — one treatment killed all lice and nits. This time I tried this one, and next day found 2 live lice and nits. Mega shocked. Went back to pharmacy, bought the other product, 4 more lice came out and then everything was dead. Wasted money.

Not recommendable. The spray bottle is a real flop — very unhandy and hard on adult hands. Because of the silicone content, the entire bathroom floor was covered in a silicone film after hair wash — dangerous slip hazard and lots of cleaning needed.
Money down the drain. Doesn't meet expectations. The lice are alive. I combed them all out but next day some were still there.

Useless, stay away. Maybe as an in-between treatment but not as first-line. It doesn't kill the critters. You have to comb them out too. We kept the product on 40 minutes, combed — next day we found more live critters. I would love to get my money back.
Doesn't help! My first 1-star review! Despite correct use including combing (which supposedly is optional) the lice are not gone. Super annoying!
No effect. Didn't do anything.

Product good, price increase highly questionable. Product really works from the first application, gentle, applies to kids and adults well, not too liquid so it doesn't run uncontrollably. 5 stars for the product itself. BUT: last bottle in February 2026 for around 15 EUR, now 1.5 months later almost 32 EUR. What justifies this? No thanks, switching to another product!

Not again. I had so much hope in this product because of all the positive reviews. Asking myself now if everyone was bought? Although you supposedly don't even need the comb, we combed meticulously until my daughter almost cried. Two days later even more nits than before. No returns possible. The product is simply junk and my money gone.

Price hammer. I bought this a few months ago for 15 EUR as emergency stock — it's top... now it costs 34 EUR?! Not normal. I'd easily need 2 bottles for my and my daughters' hair — that's nearly 70 EUR. A cheek.

Doesn't live up to its promises. After endless battles with pharmacy products we found this and were convinced by the reviews — but no. The 'only 1 application' description is misleading: this only kills adult lice, not the eggs, like all others. You need a second treatment after 8-10 days. 2 stars because it smells pleasant, is easy to apply, and cheaper than other shampoos.

Lice treatment — didn't fully convince me. Easy to apply and spread, pleasant smell, but it runs and drips. After contact time the oil washes out better than others. Unfortunately it doesn't kill all the lice. The bottle also broke down after a short time so it dripped instead of spraying.

Huge mess. The product is like oil. When sprayed it leaves greasy spots in the surroundings. During contact time the oily stuff runs and drips from hair and scalp into ears, eyes, neck. Terribly disgusting. Can't judge efficacy since we only used it as precaution.
Didn't do the job. Unfortunately didn't work.
Bad.
So-so. Smells good and leaves nice hair but I don't really know if it fully works. I have to use it twice.

Works but... we've used it several times now. Seems to work — dead lice afterwards. But: all of us have thick long curly hair. So it gets not only in the hair but all around — you have to wipe up after. Hair still very greasy after use, even with shampoo. After the 3rd shampoo round it was finally out.
Product top, bottle flop. Unfortunately the bottle arrived already oily. When it was used, the spray button broke off. Lucky those with a proper bottle! Effectiveness was very good, hence 3 stars.
Not the solution. Didn't really work. Despite use as prescribed and combing, the problem was not resolved.

Fast effect! Bought on recommendation and am really enthusiastic! Negatives first: when opening, the spray head popped out. Had to refit it. You need to watch out — product lands on the floor and makes it slippery. Positives: pleasant smell, super to spread, after one application really everything was dead, the included comb is also great.
So-so. The note on the packaging isn't accurate. I used the product 3 times at 7-day intervals and only then had peace.
It doesn't kill them. Even after 30 minutes with soaked hair, found some alive ones.

Actually great but... I bought 2 bottles in May/June because lice seem to go around at school all year. Was really enthusiastic and wanted to stock up. Unfortunately can't — in June it cost 15 EUR, now 35 EUR?? Excuse me but no. Back to the pharmacy.

Didn't deliver what was promised. Didn't help us. Easy spray application but not optimal because a lot goes into the surrounding air as mist — you inhale more than with conventional applicators. The included plastic comb is rubbish. Wouldn't buy again.

Burns the eyes. Super easy to apply, doesn't smell biting, hair combs out easily. BUT: 3 hours later my kids' eyes are still burning and you need 2 shampoo rounds to get the oily consistency out. Whether it works I'll have to see. I could comb out a lot but not with this plastic comb — I used one from another package.
Lice treatment — good but hair loss afterwards.
Meh. Didn't convince. Had to switch to another brand after all.

You have to wipe up afterwards. Spray application was easy, smell is OK. Doesn't drip off the head but unfortunately the product spreads everywhere around you — you have to clean floor, sink, mirror. For long thick hair one bottle was enough for me and my son. Worked well overall.
Does what it should, but... When spraying, a lot falls on the floor making it extremely slippery. Put a towel under the chair. The spray head is also somewhat awkward and painful on thick hair.

Applied it on my daughter. Smell is citrus-like, takes getting used to. Hair becomes greasy — definitely put a towel around the shoulders. Don't know yet if it works but I hope so. Will apply again in a week to be safe.
Helps immediately. Very good product — works fast. Easy to use thanks to the spray head and the itching stops clearly after rinsing. Only downside is the smell.

No more itching after one treatment. We had a lot of lice on my daughter who has long thick curly hair. The product is efficient and about 2/3 was used in one application. A nice comb is included — we combed out a herd of dead ones. Afterwards silence. Unfortunately one louse survived (or was reintroduced from school) and the game started again, though not to the same extent. One can doesn't last 2 treatments on long hair.
Helped well. After several applications the pests are gone.
Pleasant and easy application. Easy — press the button, spray the hair. Pleasant non-biting smell. Hair does get oily so definitely wash twice. Otherwise super — would buy again.

100% recommend. My daughter got lice from school and this liquid helped us a lot. After twice use, problem gone forever. 4 stars because it caused a little eye irritation in my daughter, but that goes away after a few hours with water.
Quite OK. We used it after a visit with head lice in the house — prevented contagion. I always keep it in reserve. When applying, everything gets sprayed. Floors very slippery. Content very oily.

Cares for hair and scalp. Hadn't had lice for 20 years and expected the worst, but no — hair and scalp were well cared for afterwards. Neither child nor man found it unpleasant. Got rid of the critters painlessly and fast.
Very effective and easy to use for kids.
Mega. Lice gone without much effort. The hammer. Even without combing — lice gone. Totally enthusiastic.

Lice alarm? This spray is the 20-minute hero magic. For me the only lice product I still buy because it delivers every time. Extreme speed — the promised 20 minutes contact time is absolutely correct. With NitWits we had one or two tired lice the next day but after another rinse they were also history. Very gentle on sensitive kids' skin, no burning, no itching. Kid-friendly scent, not as penetrating-chemical as competitors. Very oily (normal for silicone products) but washes out easily with normal shampoo. Fine-toothed plastic comb for aftercare. Easy kid-friendly application, guaranteed success in just 20 minutes.

Effective and easy to apply — clear recommendation. NitWits All-in-One spray convinces with pleasant scent and easy application. Good effect — we got rid of the lice successfully. The included combing items are OK but don't replace a classic lice comb. Overall very good value for money.

No lice and no eggs after use! I rarely review but this stuff is mega. First time with lice, bought something at the pharmacy — paid 70 EUR for the kids and me. That stuff dripped like crazy and afterwards I pulled live lice off my kids, still full of eggs. So I bought this and applied next day. Kids found it a thousand times more pleasant. Kids have nothing on their heads anymore. Even no eggs. Thrilled and grateful someone clearly thought this through — especially for the children!

Best lice treatment. Very easy handling, pleasant smell, relatively good to wash out. Self-treatment works great because the spray bottle is easy to use. Best lice product in my opinion, unfortunately not available on prescription. I always keep one in reserve for when the next lice notice comes from school.

Very effective and easy to apply. I've used the spray a few times when lice went around the kita and it has always reliably helped. The 20-minute contact time is real, the lice are truly dead and it washes out easily. Pleasant smell and skin tolerance super — no burning, no itching. Somewhat oily but that's part of these products. For me now the only lice product I still buy.
Quick & easy. Works perfectly!

Best lice treatment on the market! I have knee-length dreads and the worst diagnosis — my daughter has lice! Went to dermatologist, found 2 nits on me. Both used Nyxxa (dr prescription) — 1x and repeat after 8 days. Then started itching after 2nd treatment. Bought 2 packs of this. Although not needed, repeated after 6 days. After first application was already 24h symptom-free. The smell is noticeably better than the other product. Application and rinsing much easier — hair feels less weighed down. Thrilled and grateful to have found it! Clearly better than the competition even without dreads.
Very satisfied — helps immediately.
Very satisfied, definitely recommendable. Great effect. Exchanging the gel is a bit of a hassle but still very effective.
All-in-One lice set. Ordered for an acquaintance and she finds it 100x better than what you can buy in stores. Everything well described and it doesn't even pull when combing.
Worth it. All super — it really works very well. Only used twice.
Super — everything disappears with one application.
Works very well. Nitwits was the only thing that helped us against lice.
Best on the market. Best and only spray that works — everything from the pharmacy was junk.

Flop. This is supposed to help??? It just sticks, makes hair greasy, but despite hours of combing the head was full of nits again the next day! Used strictly per instructions. You rely on positive reviews and the result is more than disappointing. Would return it if possible.
Ineffective. Good to apply, hard to wash out, many shampoo rounds needed. Unfortunately no effect, new nits found even after several treatments.
Did not work / help. The treatment doesn't work, still lice.
Not good — I couldn't rinse it out.
No effect. Incredibly thick and greasy — one application did absolutely nothing.
We did not achieve the desired result — it had no effect on the existing lice. Not at the required level, no tangible results.
Not optimal! Just grease — I might as well have used petroleum! Crazy on white fur!

From my view the lice are only stunned. Applied it twice already — each time it starts itching again on day 2. Are the creatures just knocked out for 48 hours? Tested on my whole family 2 times. Alien re-infested 1-2 days later despite washing all clothes. The critters definitely don't die. Also strongly dries out the skin. We've found another solution now. It's not bio either, as some say — look up what biocide means.
No recommendation — it's like an oil helmet in the hair. You can wash hair several more times until the stuff is out.
Don't recommend. Unfortunately didn't help even though I gave it more time than needed.
Doesn't help. Used it twice. Absolutely nothing.
Doesn't really help. It reliably kills lice but is completely ineffective against nits (eggs). You have to do multiple treatments, same as other products. Cheaper home remedies work just as well.
Lice treatment — doesn't work.
Two hours in the hair. A bit disappointing that the serum needs to stay on for two hours. This was mentioned in the product info on Amazon.
Not Made in Germany. The included comb is really super. BUT: it's not Made in Germany but in China, clearly on the bottle. Pure lie and deception.
Parcel arrived with bottle that had leaked inside the box.

Not worth it — ruined clothes. Surprised by the positive reviews. Kids have waist-length hair. Consistency is like castor oil or honey, nearly a whole bottle per child, has to be repeated a week later. Left on 1-3 hours due to combing time. As you comb, it sprays and drips everywhere. Shower caps dripped onto clothing despite towels. Plastic sheet on the floor, nearly slipped three times. Plastic comb teeth bend — I ended up using another. Used washing-up liquid, then shampoo. Removed most nits but all tops ruined. For the 7-day follow-up I'll switch brands.
Mattes curly/wavy hair. Effective on flatmate's straight hair. Matted my own wavy/curly hair. Great when you can actually comb through it. Awful when you can't.
Not effective on thick hair. Didn't work well on my daughter's thick dark long hair. Serum so sticky and thick it was challenging to comb out. Clogged our good comb.
Very hard to rinse out. Doesn't smell bad but very hard to rinse. I don't recommend.

Lethal on hard floors. Can't comment on efficacy — used as precaution. But this is LETHAL on hard floors. My little boy slipped, another child slipped, and I slipped myself. Floors mopped but still feels oily. Don't risk it on hard floors.
Doesn't work.
Useless — didn't work at all and it's excessively greasy!
Disappointed. Expected more efficacy from the reviews. Also hard to wash out because it's too sticky.
Quality — didn't bring much despite multiple applications.
Hmm. Used 2x as instructed, unfortunately eggs and nits survived.
Like craft glue. It spreads in the hair like craft glue. You have to wash with a lot of shampoo, at least twice. But it works. Nothing can survive in that glue. Smell fairly neutral.
Meh. Wasn't fully satisfied. Didn't get everything, hard to spread and to wash out. Didn't convince us.
Lice product — very good.
Too greasy. Product probably does its job but is very greasy. Even after shampoo hair is weighed down. Less greasy products exist.
A bit expensive.
Initially good, less potent after a few uses. Good product at first but becomes less effective — maybe potency or hair getting used to it.
Sensory triggering. If you have autistic, ADHD or sensory-avoiding kids, avoid! It works but wasn't worth the meltdowns.

Messy but OK. Extremely messy — apply in the bath since the towel around shoulders made no difference. Oil based, bathroom floor slippery for weeks. Needed a second treatment the next day because hair so thick — first application didn't kill all adult lice.
Does its job — discreet.

Works. VERY viscous lice treatment. Barely runs, so not great for long hair, but great for short hair. Doesn't smell. Tip: wash out with dish soap — after two rounds it's gone. And don't forget the second treatment after ~8 days!
NitNOT lice treatment — wasn't fully satisfied.
Pretty good. Although they say you shouldn't order such products online, this is a really good product. After one application I had no more lice.
Good.
Lice — it's an oil and has to sit for 2 hours, not a shampoo.
Doesn't damage hair. Ordered for the first time, works slowly but by the end of the bottle I noticed improvement.
Wonderful for removing all the weeds from my Maltese dog.
Helps eliminate the unwanted. We've been using it with the comb for a while and it seems to have worked — but you have to use it for a while.

There's a method to killing lice and it doesn't really depend on the product. With 3 kids in school/nursery, battling lice for years, I'm resigned to treating monthly. Pros: only way to comb through my daughter's thick hair — good for thick/curly/dry hair. Cons: very time consuming — 2 hours of waiting is stressful with kids. Washing out takes forever. Other shampoo-based treatments work in 20 minutes. Most important thing is to repeat in 7 days. Recommend for specific hair types and older children.

Good. Used on my daughter who got lice at school. Worked. Left it on 1.5 hours — oily scalp, used dish soap as per instructions, then normal shampoo. After two days repeated, oil gone. Also bought the same brand's metal comb with magnifier, also good.
Nite not headlice treatment is good. Works well.
Very greasy. Killed the bits but took 4 washes to rid the grease.
Easy to use. The product is easy but very oily. Comb was very good. The problem was very difficult to wash out even with dish soap.
Excellent and recommendable. I tried many products, only this one met my expectations. Works as described. Excellent product and excellent results.
An excellent oil. Lice can't cope with it. Many times more effective than cheap oils.
Super! Delivers what it promises.
Works perfectly. Works like a charm. Better than other competition products!
Perfectly effective and really good. Very satisfied, recommend. Best of all, really perfect. The best there currently is.
Does what is needed — good.
Very good — can recommend.

Finally a product that works! After many ineffective products, Nitnot finally got rid of lice. With everything else we used, lice were still alive afterwards. With Nitnot everything was dead. I was sceptical about the long contact time with kids but it was problem-free. Washed out 2x with dish soap, then conditioner — went very well. Now they have superfine hair. Absolute recommendation!
Proven standard product — does what it should.
Really works. A bit oily but reliably kills lice. With other products we'd tried before, we had to start over every week.
Super quality.
Very good.

Package was already opened. Product arrived open — both sides of the packaging were open. You could tell it had been opened before. The bottle looks unopened since still full but cannot be ruled out that it was used and refilled with water. Will still try and update my review if it works.
Warning: no returns possible. Product arrived damaged and opened. No returns possible. Feel deceived.

Lice comb was missing. Delivery came next day. Unfortunately the package was visibly already opened. The lice comb was missing! I ordered this specifically because of the metal comb included. Can't judge efficacy.
Not good at all. Didn't work on either of my children. Wasted money. Won't buy again — went back to a well-known trusted product.
The package was damaged and the lice comb was missing — not included even if written on invoice. Had to order a lice comb on top.
Lice comb missing! Unfortunately the comb was not delivered. Annoying.

Zero effect! Never again. Used on thin short hair. Used more than half the bottle (~50ml). 45 min contact. Washed hair 8x afterwards because still oily from the stuff. Hours later found live lice. Trash!
Quality doesn't convince. Received already-opened packaging. Had to disinfect the product! Shame!
The comb is not included. Only the lotion, no comb present.
Not what was ordered — article came without comb, and twice.
Nothing.

Much too late. Don't order this product from the seller — it came several days late. When you need a lice product you need it fast. Annoying when promised delivery date isn't met. The product itself worked well — hence 2 stars.

Contrary to description no comb included! I know the product and from experience it works. Annoying that both the photo and description mention the variant with lice comb, but only the variant without comb was delivered.
Wrong product description. Warning: unlike the description and images, no comb is included.
Bald. Haven't tried it yet — described as including a comb but none in the packaging. Hence only 2 points for now.
Not so great! Had to treat my daughter's hair 4 times before all lice were gone. Not enthusiastic. Applies well and you can brush the hair well afterwards. Wouldn't buy again.

Not satisfied. Bought at the pharmacy because suitable for toddlers and pregnant women. Pharmacy recommended it. Unfortunately disappointed! Followed everything, was not stingy, 3x instead of 2x, 1 hour contact time. Combed with nit comb every day. 3 weeks after first treatment checking for control and found nits, tiny lice! Washed all textiles 3x at 60 degrees, toys in bags for 2 weeks. All the effort and 3 packs bought — wasted. 2 stars only because it doesn't stink and is easy to apply. Going back to the pharmacy tomorrow!
Not great. Good product but didn't get rid for good. Tea tree oil in conditioners and comb have worked best.

Fast application but tedious washout. The weekend after the kita note we needed something. Short 15-minute contact is really practical with impatient kids and the scalp showed no redness. Washing out was tedious — liquid very oily and several shampoo rounds needed before fully removed. The plastic comb bent quickly on my daughter's thick hair — had to use a metal one.
Better ones for handling. The product helps — that's the minimum. Applying works well, but the comb isn't very stable. For long hair the amount is too little.

Doesn't help either. In my experience this product is no more effective than pharmacy products, contrary to others' reviews here. It worked for some only because they'd already done multiple rounds with other products and combing. The amount of combing is what matters, not the product. Important: the included free comb is awful — hair gets caught in the edges and pulled out. Buy a better one separately.
It removes lice but not eggs. So must be applied more than once to completely eliminate lice.
Came without comb. Delivery came already opened and without comb. Since we urgently needed the product I couldn't return it.
Helps well. What's not so nice — hair stays super greasy for days afterwards.

Does its job. Got it as follow-up treatment because a pharmacy product only partially helped. Does actually eliminate (last) nits. But product very, very oily. Two hair washes needed after treatment (short hair). The included comb does what it should — better than a plastic model. Would buy again.

Works short-term. First days itching stopped but came back. Only applied once because I noticed increased hair loss. Probably good product with correct application, smells pleasant. Hard to get out of hair (oil film remains even after 5 washes).
Not used — OK.
Okay. Have to wash hair 2-3 times afterwards thoroughly.
Very oily. The product helped well — all the critters were dead. But I find it very, very oily. Multiple hair washes needed. Hard with girls with long hair who don't like washing.
Who is comb? I ordered three and didn't get a single lice comb. Main image says the comb should be included. In my case three.
It helped us. Definitely better than the more expensive pharmacy product. Much cheaper and more efficient. Only the comb is junk, teeth way too far apart — better order a quality one separately.

Hopefully working. Afternoon care had lice for the 6th week. Parents of the kid who brought it in aren't capable of handling it, so I bought this for the staff so the kid can be treated. Hope it works.

First impression good. We wanted an alternative to Nyda. With Nyda we're satisfied but the 8-hour contact time is a weekday problem. First impression of Linicin good — 15-min contact is a plus. But no 5 stars: daughter's long hair was very hard to wash out — two rounds did nothing, 5 shampoo rounds next day. Honey-like consistency, really hard to wash out. Shorter hair much better. Can't judge efficacy yet since second treatment.

Really effective. Tried many products that didn't live up to promises. Lice were still alive the day after other treatments, child even had allergic reaction. So we tried this sceptically. And it really worked. Lice finally gone. One star off because hair remains greasy even after shampoo. But given the efficacy, acceptable.
Thumbs up.
Satisfied. Had to use 2-3 times before it helped.
Very effective. Worked very well for me.
Lives up to its promise. Article does its job. However neither package contained a comb as described.
Helpful — was good.
Quite good. No comb included.
Good. Came today, good.
Works perfectly and quick. Easy to use and really quick!
Does its job. Bought because a friend's child had lice. After applying they were all gone. Also practical that a comb was included. Top product — clear recommendation.
Very good product.
Effective. Very effective for us. Mild smell, not as biting as some others. Short contact time is very practical.
Great — works super.

Wow! When I was little there was that horror product with fire on the packaging — I remember how it burnt the eyes! Today I can use this on my little one. Due to nursery we had 2 lice outbreaks but with this we got rid of the little monsters fast. Feels more like a care product, hair becomes super soft, easy to comb through. Would never use anything else. Daughter has very long hair. Clear recommendation!
Super product. Really helps. But afterwards everything is slippery — slip hazard.

Helped. After dealing with the pests this year and trying various things I stumbled on Linicin. Wish I'd found it earlier — this product finally helped! Very affordable. One week delivery, instructions understandable in English. Lice suffocate and nits' development disrupted. Family now lice-free after 2 months of intense combing.

For us the most effective lice product. I have 5 kids and for 3 years we had recurring lice. One daughter had them basically from age 1 to 4. Tried many products: Paranix, Licener, Rausch, SOS lice shampoo or just coconut oil. Did weekly treatments for months and lice kept coming back. Curly afro-mix hair hard to comb. Only Linicin finally freed us permanently. Most lice shampoos dry out hair — this one is very oily which actually moisturizes the kids' hair.

Helpful. Daughter first time with lice. Used a shampoo first but preferred this oil because it didn't dry out the scalp. Applied every 3 days. You have to pull out the eggs — the comb helps somewhat but not everything. The oil makes it easier with fingers. But no matter what you use, you have to check the scalp every night.
Lice product — does what it should.
Good.
Works well. Worked after one application. The comb is very useful.
